page: Amazon Web Services x1e.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 976.0 GB of system memory, providing 30.5 GB of RAM per physical core. It includes 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, operating under the Xen hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as the stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core score, fall into the bottom 10% tier. Similarly, single-threaded operations like bzip3 decompression and gzip compression are weak to poor. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by the stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register in the middle 50% tier, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

This server is configured for workloads requiring massive memory capacity relative to compute power, such as large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise resource planning applications. While the older Intel Xeon architecture limits single-threaded efficiency, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications that do not require high-frequency compute. It is appropriate for Redis deployments, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing, but less optimal for compute-heavy or single-threaded tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x1e.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications. It features 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 976.0 GB of RAM and 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Xen hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network speed of 5 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded decompression are poor, placing in the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth achieve average tier results. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks. It is configured for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, and large-scale database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 976 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for memory-intensive database workloads and caching layers.

tagline: A memory-intensive server featuring massive RAM capacity and local NVMe storage, configured for large-scale database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
976.0 GB of system memory providing 30.5 GB per core
960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Xen hypervisor
Average multi-core performance contrasted with weak single-core benchmarks
Configured for in-memory databases, Redis, and memory-bound enterprise applications
